changes_made: Changed k8s_audit_policy_captures_all_actions to k8s_audit_policy_all_actions_logged for clarity
review_notes: The functions are applicable to Kubernetes as they relate to audit logging, which is a core feature of Kubernetes security. The naming conventions mostly follow the pattern, but one function name was adjusted for clarity and consistency. The functions accurately reflect the need to ensure audit logging is enabled, comprehensive, and retained.
naming_improvements: Use 'all_actions_logged' instead of 'captures_all_actions' for better clarity and consistency
title: Examine audit log configurations and log data to verify that all actions taken by any individual ...
step1_reasoning: The control 10.2.1.2 focuses on ensuring that audit logs capture all actions taken by individuals, which directly maps to Kubernetes audit logging capabilities. Kubernetes provides an audit logging mechanism that can be configured to capture detailed information about API requests, including actions taken by users. The functions generated ensure that audit logging is enabled, configured to capture all relevant actions, and that logs are retained for compliance purposes.
